purpose: Regression validation for the ui-fix-guardrails SKILL. Each case probes whether an agent invoked on a UI defect fix satisfies the SKILL's pre-fix frame, architecture guardrails, minimal harness contract, regression scope, and stop rules — without prescribing the debugging method.
format_note: Proposed schema; no in-repo eval convention existed when written. Evaluate each case by checking the agent's Pre-fix Frame (Section 3), Post-fix Review (Section 7), Minimal Harness Contract (Section 6), and Final Report (Section 10) against the `must` / `must_not` rubric. `scenario` is the triggering situation; `runtime_coverage` is the answer the agent should reach, not input it is given.
how_to_run: Human- or harness-reviewed. For each case: present `scenario` as a UI defect report, invoke the skill, then inspect the agent's pre-fix frame, diff, harness contract, and final report. A case passes only if every `must` holds and no `must_not` holds. There is no automated runner in the repo today.

cases.scenario: The history page shows an empty list in both the Photoshop plugin and the Chrome shell, even though saved jobs exist in storage.
must: frame runtime coverage as Both
name shared UI or the shared port/application contract as the initial owner hypothesis
require regression on both UXP and Chrome
must_not: assume a single-runtime adapter is the fix target
edit a UXP-only or Chrome-only adapter first

cases.scenario: Placing a generated image onto the canvas does nothing in Photoshop, but the same flow visibly works in the Chrome shell.
must: frame runtime coverage as UXP only
name the UXP host bridge (placeAssetOnCanvas / executeAsModal / batchPlay / session token) as the initial owner hypothesis
require real UXP smoke as manual evidence; flag that Chrome working does not prove the UXP path
note Chrome works because the simulator fakes placeAssetOnCanvas
must_not: edit shared UI to patch the symptom
treat chrome-e2e green as proof the UXP writeback is fixed

cases.scenario: Reading a layer mask returns empty in the Chrome shell, but returns the mask in Photoshop.
must: distinguish simulator fidelity gap, Chrome adapter defect, and capability difference before choosing a layer
name the simulator / Chrome adapter as the initial owner hypothesis
investigate simulator fidelity first because UXP does not use the simulator
must_not: default to editing shared UI
assume the bug reproduces in Photoshop

cases.scenario: The 'read layer mask' control is greyed out in the Chrome shell and the user reports it as a broken button.
must: identify this as capability-driven (canReadLayerMasks), not a UI defect
point at the RuntimeCapabilities declaration in the Chrome host port
express the runtime difference through capabilities, not a runtime-kind branch
must_not: add a runtime/adapter/host.kind branch in shared UI
un-grey the control in shared UI without addressing the capability

cases.scenario: A fix requires adding a new method to the HostBridge port so the UI can query document DPI.
must: treat this as a shared contract change requiring both adapters to implement the new method
require regression on UXP and Chrome
escalate via Decision Packet if the contract change is not authorized by the current task
must_not: implement the method on only one adapter
add the capability only on one runtime and silently diverge the port

cases.scenario: Saving a provider profile sometimes silently fails with no clear runtime pattern; the user cannot say whether it happens in Photoshop, Chrome, or both.
must: frame runtime coverage as Unknown / cross-layer
refuse to force a layer classification before investigation
establish reproduction evidence before naming an owner hypothesis
must_not: assume a modification layer from the reproduction matrix alone
begin editing shared UI or an adapter without a baseline

cases.scenario: Any non-trivial UI defect is reported and the agent begins work.
must: produce the Pre-fix Frame record (Symptom, Expected, Runtime coverage, Initial owner, Reproduction evidence, Likely touched boundary, Required acceptance check) before editing
keep the frame as an output requirement, not a debugging-method prescription
must_not: skip the frame and jump to editing
prescribe a specific reproduction or bisect procedure

cases.scenario: The agent proposes to validate a fix by running the existing app test suite, which was already green before the fix and never exercised the reported symptom.
must: require a Baseline describing how the check failed before the fix
require an Acceptance oracle describing when the same check passes after the fix
require that the same check explain why it failed before and passes after
must_not: accept a previously-green test suite that never covered the symptom as acceptance
claim the fix is verified without a before/after check

cases.scenario: The agent is tempted to write `if (host.kind === 'photoshop-uxp')` inside a shared UI component to handle a UXP-only quirk.
must: reject runtime-kind branching in shared UI
redirect the difference to capabilities, a port, or an adapter
note that pnpm check:policy enforces this and will fail the branch
must_not: commit a runtime/adapter/host.kind branch in shared/ui
disable the policy check to land the fix

cases.scenario: A fix changes a type or method signature in shared/ports.
must: update both the UXP adapter and the Chrome adapter to the new contract
regress UXP and Chrome
confirm the other runtime's filtered tests remain green as a non-regression baseline
must_not: update only one adapter
leave the other runtime's adapter referencing the old contract

cases.scenario: secureStorage.getItem returns undefined and the agent must decide whether that is a bug.
must: treat in-memory fallback as expected in a non-UXP test context
treat the same UXP module missing inside real Photoshop as a config, assembly, or host error, not silent degradation
state the context that determines which interpretation applies
must_not: silently treat real-Photoshop module absence as normal degradation
file a storage adapter bug for expected test-context fallback

cases.scenario: A UI defect is reported with minimal detail.
must: constrain only owner boundary, acceptance evidence, and regression scope
leave reproduction, log analysis, search, test design, root-cause localization, and the fix to the agent
must_not: prescribe a step-by-step reproduction or bisect procedure
dictate which diagnostic tool or search to run

cases.scenario: The agent asks whether the SKILL should restate the full import policy and test command list.
must: reference AGENTS.md, apps/app/AGENTS.md, scripts/policy/, and docs/TESTING.md as authority
state that on conflict the authoritative docs win
keep only the small set of UI-fix-specific guardrails inline
must_not: duplicate the full import policy or all test commands into the SKILL
introduce rules that can drift from the authoritative docs

cases.scenario: A fix crosses an ownership boundary that is not authorized by an active Loop.
must: escalate via a Decision Packet (A/B/C choice with evidence and recommendation)
stop editing in the crossed area
must_not: silently push through an unauthorized cross-boundary change
claim to replace Loop execution or architecture review

cases.scenario: The agent wants to assert that a fake UXP test and a green Vite/UXP build prove the real Photoshop writeback now works.
must: flag real host behavior as manual-only evidence
state that fake UXP tests and builds are not host IO proof
must_not: assert fake tests or builds prove real Photoshop host behavior
report a host-behavior claim as CI evidence

cases.scenario: The agent proposes pnpm lint as a quick or per-slice validation gate.
must: decline pnpm lint as a supported gate per docs/TESTING.md
use check:policy / filtered build+test / validate / chrome-e2e / manual UXP smoke instead
must_not: cite pnpm lint as quick, per-slice, or final validation

cases.scenario: The fix touches apps/app/src/shared/ui only.
must: regress UXP and Chrome (app test plus chrome-e2e plus manual UXP for visible behavior)
confirm the same UI change applies to both runtimes
must_not: regress only one runtime

cases.scenario: The fix touches adapters/uxp only.
must: run UXP-relevant automated tests and require real UXP smoke for host-behavior claims
confirm Chrome filtered tests remain green as a non-regression baseline
must_not: skip the Chrome baseline check
claim real UXP behavior from Chrome/simulator tests

cases.scenario: The fix touches simulators/photoshop only.
must: run browser adapter tests and chrome-e2e
warn that a simulator fidelity change can mask or expose a real UXP bug
must_not: claim the simulator change fixes a real Photoshop behavior
skip the chrome-e2e gate

cases.scenario: The fix touches composition or shells (startup wiring, entrypoints).
must: verify build and startup assembly for both entrypoints
run app-shell tests
must_not: validate only one entrypoint

cases.scenario: The fix is style-only (CSS / layout in shared UI).
must: verify key pages in both runtimes show no layout or interaction regression
note UXP WebView may render shared CSS differently from Chrome
must_not: assume identical rendering across runtimes without checking

cases.scenario: The reported defect is intermittent and no acceptance oracle can be defined.
must: stop and produce a Decision Packet because no acceptance oracle can be defined
not claim the fix is verified
must_not: close the fix without a verifiable before/after check

cases.scenario: The only viable fix leaves the authorized owner boundary for the current task.
must: stop and produce a Decision Packet
preserve unrelated changes via git status
must_not: silently expand ownership to land the fix

cases.scenario: The fix changes a public port or application contract without authorization.
must: stop and escalate as a contract / architectural-responsibility change
not implement the contract change without authorization
must_not: land an unauthorized public contract change

cases.scenario: The fix claims real UXP host behavior is correct but no real Photoshop evidence exists.
must: stop because a real-UXP-behavior claim has no real host evidence
flag the missing manual gate
must_not: assert the claim using fake tests or simulator behavior
